purpose: Master reference for all teaching strategies per operation, per difficulty level, per grade. Every hint in the app draws from this file.

single_digit_to_10.name: Addition to 10 (single digit)
single_digit_to_10.strategy: Count On from the Bigger Number
single_digit_to_10.when_to_use: Any addition where both numbers are 0-10 and the sum is 10 or less
steps: Find which number is BIGGER
Put the bigger number IN YOUR HEAD
Show the smaller number on your FINGERS
Count on from the bigger number, folding one finger each count
When all fingers are folded, that's your answer!

example.voice_script: Which number is bigger? 5! Put 5 in your head. Now show 3 fingers. Count on from 5... 6, 7, 8! The answer is 8!

add_0.voice: 7 plus 0? When you add zero, the number stays the same! 7 + 0 = 7!

add_1.voice: 6 plus 1? That's just the next number after 6... which is 7!

doubles.voice: 4 plus 4? That's a double! Think of 4 fingers on each hand. Count them all: 8!

single_digit_to_20.name: Addition to 20 (single digit, sums 11-20)
single_digit_to_20.when_to_use: When two single-digit numbers add up to more than 10
single_digit_to_20.why_not_fingers: We only have 10 fingers! So we need a smarter strategy.
steps: Look at both numbers. We need to MAKE 10 first.
Ask: how many more does the BIGGER number need to reach 10?
Take that many from the smaller number
Now you have 10 + whatever is left
10 + anything is easy — just put a 1 in front!

example.voice_script: 7 plus 8. Let's make 10 first! 8 needs 2 more to reach 10. Take 2 from the 7, leaving 5. Now we have 10 + 5 = 15!

alternative.name: Near Doubles
alternative.voice: 7 plus 8? I know 7 + 7 = 14. And 8 is just one more than 7. So 14 + 1 = 15!

double_digit_plus_single.name: Double digit + single digit
double_digit_plus_single.strategy: Add the Ones, Keep the Tens
double_digit_plus_single.when_to_use: When adding a 1-digit number to a 2-digit number (e.g., 34 + 5)
steps: Look at the two-digit number. Split it into TENS and ONES
Add the single digit to the ONES part
If the ones add up to less than 10, just put it back with the tens
If the ones add up to 10 or more, carry 1 to the tens

example_no_carry.voice_script: 34 plus 5. Split 34: that's 30 and 4. Now add the ones: 4 + 5 = 9. Put it back: 30 + 9 = 39!

example_with_carry.voice_script: 37 plus 6. Split 37: that's 30 and 7. Add the ones: 7 + 6 = 13. That's more than 10! 13 is 10 + 3. Carry the 10 to the tens: 30 + 10 = 40. Answer: 40 + 3 = 43!

double_digit_plus_double.name: Double digit + double digit
double_digit_plus_double.strategy: Stack and Add Columns (right to left)
steps: Write the numbers stacked on top of each other
Line up the ONES column and the TENS column
Add the ONES column first (right side)
If the ones total is 10 or more, write the ones digit and CARRY the 1
Add the TENS column (plus any carry)
Write the tens digit. That's your answer!

example_no_carry.voice_script: Stack them up! Ones first: 2 + 5 = 7, write 7. Tens next: 4 + 3 = 7, write 7. Answer: 77!

example_with_carry.voice_script: Stack them up! Ones first: 8 + 6 = 14. Write 4, carry the 1. Tens: 4 + 3 = 7, plus the carried 1 = 8. Answer: 84!

triple_digit.name: Triple digit addition (to 1000)
steps: Stack the numbers, line up ones, tens, hundreds
Add ONES first (right column). Carry if 10+
Add TENS next (middle column + carry). Carry if 10+
Add HUNDREDS last (left column + carry)
Read the answer!

example.voice_script: Ones: 7 + 5 = 12. Write 2, carry 1. Tens: 4 + 8 = 12, plus 1 = 13. Write 3, carry 1. Hundreds: 3 + 2 = 5, plus 1 = 6. Answer: 632!

single_digit_to_10.name: Subtraction within 10
single_digit_to_10.strategy: Count Up from the Smaller Number
single_digit_to_10.when_to_use: Any subtraction where both numbers are 10 or less
steps: Put the SMALLER number in your head
Count UP to the BIGGER number on your fingers
How many fingers did you hold up? That's the answer!

example.voice_script: 9 minus 4. Put 4 in your head. Now count up to 9 on your fingers: 5, 6, 7, 8, 9! You held up 5 fingers. So 9 - 4 = 5!

subtract_0.voice: 8 minus 0? Taking away nothing means the number stays the same! 8!

subtract_itself.voice: 6 minus 6? Take away all of them... nothing left! 0!

subtract_1.voice: 7 minus 1? That's just the number right before 7... which is 6!

single_digit_to_20.name: Subtraction within 20
single_digit_to_20.when_to_use: When subtracting from a teen number (11-20)
steps: Break the bigger number into 10 and the rest
Subtract from 10 first if the ones digit is too small
Add back whatever ones were left

example.voice_script: 15 minus 8. Break 15 into 10 and 5. Now, 10 minus 8 is easy: that's 2! Add back the 5: 2 + 5 = 7! So 15 - 8 = 7!

alternative.name: Count Up
alternative.voice: 15 minus 8? Start at 8, count up to 15: 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15. That's 7 counts! Answer: 7!

double_digit_minus_single.name: Double digit - single digit
double_digit_minus_single.strategy: Subtract the Ones, Keep the Tens
double_digit_minus_single.when_to_use: Subtracting a 1-digit number from a 2-digit number

example_no_borrow.voice_script: 47 minus 3. The tens stay: 40. Subtract the ones: 7 - 3 = 4. Put it back: 40 + 4 = 44!

example_with_borrow.voice_script: 43 minus 7. The ones: 3 minus 7? Can't do it! Borrow 10 from the tens. 40 becomes 30, and 3 becomes 13. Now 13 - 7 = 6. Answer: 30 + 6 = 36!

double_digit_minus_double.name: Double digit - double digit
double_digit_minus_double.strategy: Stack and Subtract Columns (right to left)
steps: Stack the numbers (bigger on top)
Subtract ONES column first
If top ones is smaller, BORROW 10 from the tens
Subtract TENS column (minus any borrowed)

example.voice_script: Stack them! Ones: 2 minus 8? Can't! Borrow from tens. 7 tens becomes 6 tens, and 2 becomes 12. Now 12 - 8 = 4. Tens: 6 - 3 = 3. Answer: 34!

triple_digit.name: Triple digit subtraction
triple_digit.strategy: Stack and Subtract 3 Columns with Borrowing

example.voice_script: Ones: 3 minus 7? Borrow! 13 - 7 = 6. Tens: 1 minus 8? Borrow again! 11 - 8 = 3. Hundreds: 4 - 2 = 2. Answer: 236!

concept_intro.name: What IS multiplication?
teaching_sequence: Step 1: Show GROUPS of objects — 'How many altogether?'
Step 2: Introduce the × sign — it means 'groups of'
Step 4: Introduce ARRAYS — rows and columns they can draw
Step 5: Build toward skip counting through repeated adding

single_digit_1_to_5.name: Times tables 1-5
single_digit_1_to_5.when_to_use: Multiplying any two numbers where both are 1-5
steps: Think: the first number tells you HOW MANY GROUPS
The second number tells you HOW MANY IN EACH GROUP
Draw the groups (or imagine them)
Use repeated addition: add the group size that many times
For ×1: the number stays the same (1 group of anything = that thing)
For ×2: it's doubles! You already know doubles from addition!
For ×5: count by 5s (like counting nickels)

example.voice_script: 4 times 3 means 4 groups of 3. Let's add: 3 plus 3 is 6. Plus 3 more is 9. Plus 3 more is 12! So 4 × 3 = 12!

tricks.times_1: Any number × 1 = itself. 7 × 1 = 7. One group of 7 is just 7!
tricks.times_0: Any number × 0 = 0. Zero groups of anything = nothing!

single_digit_6_to_10.name: Times tables 6-10
single_digit_6_to_10.when_to_use: When one or both numbers are 6-10
single_digit_6_to_10.why: Groups get big — 8 groups of 7 is hard to count! So we break it into parts we already know.
steps: Split the BIGGER number into two parts you know
Multiply each part separately
Add the two results together

example.voice_script: 7 times 6. Let's break 6 into 5 and 1. We know 7 × 5 = 35 (count by 5s!). And 7 × 1 = 7. Now add: 35 + 7 = 42! So 7 × 6 = 42!

alternative.voice_script: 8 times 7. Break 7 into 5 and 2. We know 8 × 5 = 40 and 8 × 2 = 16. Add: 40 + 16 = 56!

tricks.times_9: 9 × any number: multiply by 10 then subtract the number. 9 × 7 = 70 - 7 = 63!

double_digit.name: Double digit multiplication
double_digit.when_to_use: Multiplying a double-digit number by a single digit
steps: Split the double-digit number into TENS and ONES
Multiply the TENS part by the single digit
Multiply the ONES part by the single digit
ADD the two results together

example.voice_script: 14 times 3. Break 14 into 10 and 4. Now: 10 × 3 = 30. And 4 × 3 = 12. Add them: 30 + 12 = 42! So 14 × 3 = 42!

concept_intro.name: What IS division?
teaching_sequence: Step 1: SHARING — 'Deal cookies to friends equally'
Step 2: The ÷ sign means 'shared equally among' or 'split into groups of'
Step 3: Connect to multiplication — 'Division asks: what × 3 = 12?'
Step 4: Repeated subtraction — 'Keep taking away groups and count how many times'

simple_facts_1_to_5.name: Division by 1-5
simple_facts_1_to_5.when_to_use: Dividing where the divisor is 1-5 and answers are within the times tables
steps: Think: 'I have THIS MANY things to share among THIS MANY groups'
Ask yourself: 'What number TIMES the divisor gives me the big number?'
If stuck: draw circles for groups, deal dots one by one, count per group

example.voice_script: 15 divided by 3. Think: what times 3 equals 15? Let's count by 3s: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15! That's 5 counts. So 15 ÷ 3 = 5! Check: 5 × 3 = 15. Correct!

fallback_for_young_kids.voice: 15 divided by 3? Draw 3 circles. Now deal 15 dots, one to each circle, going around. Count how many each circle got: 5! Answer: 5!

tricks.divide_by_1: Any number ÷ 1 = itself. 9 ÷ 1 = 9. One group gets everything!
tricks.divide_by_itself: Any number ÷ itself = 1. 7 ÷ 7 = 1. Each person gets exactly one!
tricks.zero_divided: 0 ÷ any number = 0. Zero cookies shared among friends = nobody gets any!

facts_6_to_10.name: Division by 6-10
steps: Ask: 'What times THIS number gives me THAT number?'
Use the multiplication facts you already know!
If stuck: use repeated subtraction — keep subtracting the divisor and count how many times

example.voice_script: 56 divided by 8. Think: what times 8 equals 56? I know 7 × 8 = 56! So 56 ÷ 8 = 7!

fallback.voice: 56 ÷ 8? Keep subtracting 8: 56, 48, 40, 32, 24, 16, 8, 0. Count: that's 7 times! Answer: 7!

double_digit_dividend.name: Dividing larger numbers
double_digit_dividend.strategy: Break Apart and Divide Each Part
double_digit_dividend.when_to_use: When dividing a double-digit number that can be split
steps: Break the big number into a TENS part and a ONES part that are both easy to divide
Divide each part separately
Add the results

example.voice_script: 84 divided by 4. Break 84 into 80 and 4. 80 ÷ 4 = 20. And 4 ÷ 4 = 1. Add: 20 + 1 = 21! So 84 ÷ 4 = 21!

count_by_2s.name: Count by 2s
count_by_2s.strategy: Each number is 2 more than the last
count_by_2s.voice: We're counting by 2s! Like counting pairs of shoes: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10...
count_by_2s.backward: Going backward by 2s means subtract 2 each time

count_by_5s.name: Count by 5s
count_by_5s.strategy: Each number is 5 more — like counting nickels!
count_by_5s.voice: Count by 5s like counting nickels! 5, 10, 15, 20, 25... The ones digit goes 5, 0, 5, 0!

count_by_10s.name: Count by 10s
count_by_10s.strategy: Just change the tens digit — easiest skip counting!
count_by_10s.voice: 10, 20, 30, 40... just change the first number and keep the 0!

find_pattern.name: Find the Pattern
find_pattern.strategy: Find the RULE — how much does each number change by?
steps: Look at the first two numbers. What's the difference?
Check: does the same difference work for the next pair?
Apply the rule to find the missing number

split_test.name: Split Test
split_test.strategy: Can you split the number into 2 EQUAL groups? If yes = EVEN, if 1 left over = ODD
split_test.voice: Try splitting it! 6 splits into 3 and 3 — nothing left over. EVEN! 7 splits into 3 and 3 with 1 left. ODD!

last_digit_rule.name: Last Digit Rule
last_digit_rule.strategy: For bigger numbers, just look at the LAST digit. If it's 0,2,4,6,8 = EVEN. If 1,3,5,7,9 = ODD.
last_digit_rule.voice: The last digit tells you everything! 48 ends in 8 — EVEN! 73 ends in 3 — ODD!

tens_and_ones.name: Tens and Ones
tens_and_ones.strategy: Left digit = tens, right digit = ones. In 47: 4 tens (40) and 7 ones (7).
tens_and_ones.voice: Every digit has a PLACE. The left digit is tens — it tells you how many groups of 10. The right digit is ones.

hundreds_tens_ones.name: Hundreds, Tens, Ones
hundreds_tens_ones.strategy: Three digits: leftmost = hundreds, middle = tens, rightmost = ones.
hundreds_tens_ones.voice: In 356: 3 is hundreds (300), 5 is tens (50), 6 is ones (6). 300 + 50 + 6 = 356!

expanded_form.name: Expanded Form
expanded_form.strategy: Break number into parts showing the value of each digit

scripture_bank.purpose: Encouragement verses shown on wrong answers — different every time

verses.text: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid.
The Lord is my helper, I will not fear.
With God all things are possible.
Trust in the Lord with all thine heart.
The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.
For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love.
The joy of the Lord is your strength.
Commit thy way unto the Lord; trust also in him.
I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.
In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
The Lord is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble.
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
Cast thy burden upon the Lord, and he shall sustain thee.
Delight thyself also in the Lord; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
Ye are the light of the world.
But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength.
For we walk by faith, not by sight.
Be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only.
A merry heart doeth good like a medicine.
Let your light so shine before men.
Great is thy faithfulness.
This is the day which the Lord hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
Children, obey your parents in the Lord: for this is right.
Train up a child in the way he should go.
The Lord bless thee, and keep thee.
Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above.
The earth is the Lord's, and the fulness thereof.
A soft answer turneth away wrath.
Be still, and know that I am God.
Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
He hath made every thing beautiful in his time.
For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I.
The Lord is nigh unto all them that call upon him.
Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
O give thanks unto the Lord; for he is good.
The Lord is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?
And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God.
If God be for us, who can be against us?
Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it.
The Lord thy God in the midst of thee is mighty.
For with God nothing shall be impossible.
He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
Draw nigh to God, and he will draw nigh to you.
Whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord.
Let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap.
The Lord is gracious, and full of compassion.
Seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness.
Jesus said, Let the little children come unto me.
